evals.prompt: Show me all sign-ins from IP 203.0.113.42 in the last 7 days, including whether they succeeded.
evals.expected_output: A KQL query using ipv6_is_match() for the IP comparison (not == string equality), querying EntraIdSignInEvents or AADSignInEventsBeta, with a 7-day time filter and relevant projected columns.

assertions.name: uses_ipv6_is_match_not_equals
assertions.description: Query uses ipv6_is_match() for IP comparison, not == or contains or has

assertions.name: has_time_filter
assertions.description: Query includes a time filter (ago(7d) or similar)

assertions.name: queries_signin_table
assertions.description: Query uses a sign-in table (EntraIdSignInEvents or AADSignInEventsBeta)

evals.prompt: I want to hunt for any AI agents in our tenant that have external knowledge sources or are connected to other agents. What does that look like?
evals.expected_output: The assistant should call get_hunting_schema for AIAgentsInfo, do a take 3 sample query, then write a hunting query targeting KnowledgeDetails and ConnectedAgentsSchemaNames columns. Should not guess column semantics without sampling first.

assertions.name: calls_schema_for_AIAgentsInfo
assertions.description: Assistant calls get_hunting_schema with AIAgentsInfo before writing the main query

assertions.name: samples_table_first
assertions.description: Assistant runs a take 3 (or take N) sample on AIAgentsInfo before the main query

assertions.name: addresses_knowledge_or_connected
assertions.description: Final query targets KnowledgeDetails or ConnectedAgentsSchemaNames

evals.prompt: Find all devices that have made network connections to any IP in the 185.220.0.0/16 range (known Tor exit node range) in the past 2 weeks.
evals.expected_output: A KQL query against DeviceNetworkEvents using ipv6_is_match with the CIDR range, a 14-day time filter, and appropriate projection/summarization. Must NOT use string comparison for the IP range.

assertions.name: uses_ipv6_is_match_for_cidr
assertions.description: Query uses ipv6_is_match for CIDR range comparison, not startswith or contains

assertions.name: correct_time_filter
assertions.description: Query uses 14d or 2w time filter (not default 7d, since user specified 2 weeks)

evals.prompt: Write a KQL query to find all internet-exposed servers in ExposureGraphNodes. I want to filter NodeProperties to show only nodes that have internet exposure flagged. Show me the node name, type, and the relevant exposure properties.
evals.expected_output: Claude attempts a doc fetch for ExposureGraphNodes before writing KQL. The query uses parse_json/tostring on NodeProperties and filters for internet-exposure-related keys.
expectations: A doc fetch was attempted for ExposureGraphNodes — transcript shows a call to microsoft_docs_fetch with a learn.microsoft.com URL, OR a web fetch to raw.githubusercontent.com/MicrosoftDocs/defender-docs mentioning exposuregraphnodes, OR a web_read/WebFetch call to a learn.microsoft.com URL containing 'exposuregraphnodes'
The response includes a KQL query that references the ExposureGraphNodes table
The KQL query uses dynamic column access such as parse_json(), tostring(), or bag_keys() to work with NodeProperties

evals.prompt: Can you write a KQL query against AIAgentsInfo to list all Copilot Studio agents that have tools with external HTTP connectors? I'm doing a security review and worried about data exfiltration paths through AI agents.
evals.expected_output: Claude attempts a doc fetch for AIAgentsInfo before writing KQL. The query handles the sparse/snapshot nature of the table (uses ago(90d) or no time filter) and accesses AgentToolsDetails dynamically.
expectations: A doc fetch was attempted for AIAgentsInfo — transcript shows a call to microsoft_docs_fetch with a learn.microsoft.com URL, OR a web fetch to raw.githubusercontent.com/MicrosoftDocs/defender-docs mentioning aiagentsinfo, OR a web_read/WebFetch call to a learn.microsoft.com URL containing 'aiagentsinfo'
The response includes a KQL query that references the AIAgentsInfo table
The query either omits the time filter or uses a wide window (ago(30d) or larger) rather than the default 7d — reflecting the sparse/snapshot nature of AIAgentsInfo data

evals.prompt: I need a KQL query to hunt for suspicious OAuth app activity in CloudAppEvents — specifically looking for OAuth apps that accessed significantly more data or new resource types compared to their recent baseline. This is for an insider threat investigation.
evals.expected_output: Claude attempts a doc fetch for CloudAppEvents before writing KQL. The query aggregates by app/resource and uses dynamic column access for CloudAppEvents-specific fields.
expectations: A doc fetch was attempted for CloudAppEvents — transcript shows a call to microsoft_docs_fetch with a learn.microsoft.com URL, OR a web fetch to raw.githubusercontent.com/MicrosoftDocs/defender-docs mentioning cloudappevents, OR a web_read/WebFetch call to a learn.microsoft.com URL containing 'cloudappevents'
The response includes a KQL query that references the CloudAppEvents table
The KQL query uses aggregation (summarize) to compare baseline vs. recent activity rather than returning raw rows
